subject: Science
chapter: The World of Metals and Non-metals

activities_projects.title: Oxide Nature Test
activities_projects.objective: Determine the acidic or basic nature of metallic and non-metallic oxides
steps: Burn magnesium ribbon and dissolve the ash in water; test with red litmus
Burn a small amount of sulphur in a deflagrating spoon, collect the gas in a jar with a little water, and test with blue litmus
Compare the results of both tests
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Concludes that metal oxides are basic and non-metal oxides are acidic
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of oxide nature and litmus color changes (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Displacement Series Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Observe how a more reactive metal displaces a less reactive metal
steps: Dissolve copper sulphate in water to get a blue solution
Clean an iron nail with sandpaper and suspend it in the solution using thread
Wait for 30 minutes and observe the color change of the solution and the nail
Explain the reaction in terms of reactivity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the concept of reactivity and displacement reactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Clear description of the change from blue to green and the brown coating (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Metal Reaction with Acids
activities_projects.objective: Compare the rate of hydrogen gas production in different metals
steps: Place samples of Zinc, Iron, and Copper in separate test tubes
Add 5ml of dilute HCl to each
Observe the intensity of bubbles (effervescence) in each tube
Rank the metals from most reactive to least reactive based on bubble production
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that metals react with acids to produce salt and hydrogen gas at different rates
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct ranking of metals and gas identification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Conductivity of Graphite
activities_projects.objective: Prove that some non-metals can conduct electricity
steps: Set up a simple circuit using the battery and bulb
Connect the two exposed graphite ends of the pencil into the circuit gap
Observe if the bulb glows
Research why graphite is an exception among non-metals
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies graphite as a non-metal conductor of electricity
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful circuit completion and explanation of the exception (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Prevention of Corrosion
activities_projects.objective: Test the effectiveness of different coatings in preventing rust
steps: Apply different coatings (oil, paint, none, zinc) to four different nails
Place all nails in a tray of salt water for two days
Examine which nail shows the most and least amount of rust
Discuss which method is best for industrial use
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates various methods of preventing oxidation in metals
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparative report on the effectiveness of coatings (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Density Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Compare the mass-to-volume ratio of metals and non-metals
materials: Piece of wood/cork of similar size
steps: Measure the mass of both objects using a balance
Use the water displacement method to find the volume of each
Calculate density (Mass/Volume) for both
Note the significant difference between the metal and the non-metal
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that metals generally have higher densities than non-metals
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct density calculations and comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Reaction with Bases
activities_projects.objective: Observe the reaction of aluminium with a strong base
steps: Prepare a fresh solution of Sodium Hydroxide in a test tube
Drop a small piece of aluminium foil into it
Observe the reaction and test the evolved gas with a burning matchstick
Record the 'pop' sound
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that some metals react with bases to produce hydrogen gas
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful observation and gas verification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Alloy Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Investigate why we mix metals to form alloys
steps: Compare the iron nail with the steel spoon for rust and hardness
Compare the copper wire with the brass key for strength
Research the composition of Stainless Steel and Brass
Explain why alloys are often more useful than pure metals
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding the purpose and properties of alloys
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed notes on the advantages of alloys over pure metals (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Non-metal Displacement
steps: Add a few drops of chlorine water to the potassium iodide solution
Observe the color change as iodine is displaced
Explain the order of reactivity among non-metals
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that displacement reactions also occur among non-metals
activities_projects.evaluation: Interpretation of color change and reactivity logic (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Metal' Crossword Creator
activities_projects.objective: Synthesize chapter concepts through puzzle design
steps: Design a crossword where all answers are properties or names of metals/non-metals
Write challenging clues (e.g., 'A metal that can be cut with a knife')
Exchange with a classmate to solve
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Reinforces terminology and specific elemental properties
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of clues and puzzle structure (10 marks)
